summary: O'Neil recommends the website StemFeminist.com, which collects anonymous stories about everyday sexism in STEM fields. She notes that the stories range from seemingly minor incidents to outrageous, actionable ones, and many resonate with her own experiences or those of her friends. She argues the site serves as valuable evidence when men question the level of sexism in STEM, expressing disbelief that such issues persist in 2014.
key_points: StemFeminist.com aggregates anonymous accounts of everyday sexism in STEM fields
The experiences range widely in severity, from subtle to clearly actionable
The site provides concrete evidence to counter skepticism about sexism in STEM
Many stories resonate with O'Neil's personal and secondhand experiences
money_quotes: It consists of a bunch of anonymously contributed stories, most but not all by women, about everyday sexism that happens in the STEM fields.
It's a great idea to have this, if just for women to be able to point to when men question the level of sexism in STEM fields.
Sometimes it's hard to believe it's 2014.
key_insight: Documenting everyday sexism in STEM provides necessary evidence against those who downplay or deny its prevalence.
